rulings.Case Title: DAWN O'NEILL, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al VS. SYD CARPENTER et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 2, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Mathers was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Dawn O'Neill and Penny Rushing and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. The deposition testimony of George Kirk does not sustain defendant's burden of demonstrating that the Kaiser Gypsum powder joint compound to which Mr. Mathers was exposed did not contain asbestos. Plaintiff's objections to the deposition testimony of George Kirk are overruled. However, even if defendant had sustained its burden, the deposition testimony of Dawn O'Neill and Penny Rushing and defendant's responses to General Order No. 129 interrogatories would create a triable issue whether Mr. Mathers was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (joint compound) attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 3, Defendant Pioneer Insulation Contractor, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 2 and granted as to issues 3, 4, 5 and 6. As to summary judgment (issue 1) and summary adjudication issue 2 (duty), defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Tremblay was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant and that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Tremblay. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability against defendant in this action for survival and wrongful death as opposed to Mr. Tremblay's claims in his personal injury action. As to summary adjudication issues 3, 4, 5 and 6 (premises liability, product liability, false representation/intentional tort and punitive damages), plaintiffs waive those causes of action and claims against this defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 3, Defendant California Drywall Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to Issues 1, 2 and 3 and denied as to Issues 4 and 5. As to strict liability (Issue 1), defendant sustained its burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant can be liable under a strict liability theory. As to intentional tort and plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages (Issues 2 and 3), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant can be liable for intentional tort or punitive damages. Further, plaintiffs did not oppose summary adjudication of these claims as they were not addressed in plaintiffs' opposition. As to all duty-related summary adjudication issues (Issues 4 and 5), defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to submit evidence to negate the duty element from plaintiffs' causes of action Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Tremblay, precluding defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs' objections to decedent's deposition testimony and the declaration of Mr. Bowles are overruled. The Court did not consider Mr. Templin's declaration as it's irrelevant to the issues raised by defendant's motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 3, Defendant J.S. Guerin & Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Thomas Tremblay's deposition testimony and Mr. Templin's declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Tremblay was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (joint compound) attributable to defendant. As to defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication, it is denied in its entirety for the same reasons stated above. The only ground upon which defendant moved for each summary adjudication issue, as stated in its Notice of Motion and repeated in its Separate Statement, was identical to the ground upon which it moved for summary judgment. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 3, Defendant Aderholt Specialty Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 1 and 4 and granted as to Issues 2, 3 and 5. As to Issue 1 (duty), defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to submit evidence to negate the duty element from plaintiffs' causes of action. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Tremblay, precluding defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to Issue 2 (Strict Liability and False Representation) defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant can be liable under the strict liability and false representation causes of action. Further, plaintiffs did not oppose summary adjudication of their strict liability cause of action. As to Issue 3 (Intentional Tort), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant can be liable for intentional tort. As to Issue 4 (Premise Owner/Contractor Liability), defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that this cause of action fails even if defendant did not own, lease, manage or operate any of the premises at issue. As to Issue 5 (punitive damages), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Mr. Aderholt are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
